skill.name: ios-application-dev
skill.description:
iOS application development guide covering UIKit, SnapKit, and SwiftUI. Includes touch targets, safe areas, navigation patterns, Dynamic Type, Dark Mode, accessibility, collection views, common UI components, and SwiftUI design guidelines. For detailed references on specific topics, see the reference files.
Use when: developing iOS apps, implementing UI, reviewing iOS code, working with UIKit/SnapKit/SwiftUI layouts, building iPhone interfaces, Swift mobile development, Apple HIG compliance, iOS accessibility implementation.
skill.summary: Comprehensive iOS development guide covering UIKit, SwiftUI, accessibility, and Apple Human Interface Guidelines
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: All 674 static analyzer findings are false positives. The scanner misidentifies markdown inline code delimiters (backticks) as shell commands, Swift enum values like .hierarchical as crypto algorithms, and standard Swift guard statements as system reconnaissance. This is a legitimate iOS development reference guide containing only documentation and example code with no security risks.

content.user_title: Build Production-Ready iOS Apps with UIKit and SwiftUI
content.value_statement: Creating iOS interfaces requires understanding Apple platform conventions, accessibility standards, and proper UI patterns. This skill provides comprehensive guidance on building apps that meet Apple Human Interface Guidelines.
actual_capabilities: Generate UIKit layouts using SnapKit with proper Auto Layout constraints
Create SwiftUI views following Apple Human Interface Guidelines
Implement accessibility features including VoiceOver, Dynamic Type, and Reduce Motion
Build navigation patterns with UINavigationController, TabView, and modal presentations
Apply semantic colors and Dark Mode support in both UIKit and SwiftUI
Reference Swift coding standards for protocols, async/await, and error handling
limitations: Cannot compile or run Swift code directly
Does not provide project setup or Xcode configuration
Cannot debug runtime issues or crash reports
Does not include App Store submission guidance

use_cases.title: Scaffold iOS Screen Layouts
use_cases.description: Generate complete screen implementations with proper safe area handling, touch targets, and semantic layouts following Apple guidelines.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile developers building new iOS screens

use_cases.title: Implement Accessibility Features
use_cases.description: Add VoiceOver labels, Dynamic Type support, and Reduce Motion compatibility to existing iOS interfaces.

use_cases.title: Review iOS Code Quality
use_cases.description: Validate Swift code against Apple best practices for protocols, error handling, and UI implementation patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Create UIKit Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [screen type] screen in UIKit with SnapKit. Include [components]. Follow 44pt touch target minimum, support Dynamic Type, and use semantic colors for Dark Mode support.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Creating a new iOS screen

prompt_templates.title: Build SwiftUI View
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a SwiftUI [view type] with [functionality]. Use NavigationStack for navigation, apply semantic colors, and ensure accessibility labels are present.

prompt_templates.title: Add Accessibility Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Add VoiceOver accessibility support to [component]. Include proper accessibilityLabel, accessibilityHint, and trait configuration. Support Dynamic Type up to accessibility sizes.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Navigation Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design navigation flow for [feature]. Use TabView for main sections, NavigationStack for drill-down, and .sheet for focused tasks. Preserve state across navigation.

output_examples.input: Create a settings screen in SwiftUI with email and password fields
output_examples.output: A complete SwiftUI settings screen with Form, Section grouping, TextField for email with keyboard type and autocapitalization settings, SecureField for password, and Sign In button with disabled state when fields are empty.

output_examples.input: Add accessibility to a list of products in UIKit
output_examples.output: UIKit table view cell configuration with accessibilityLabel describing product name and price, accessibilityHint for tap action, and trait settings for button behavior. Includes Dynamic Type support with UIFontMetrics scaling.

output_examples.input: Implement Dark Mode in an existing UIKit app
output_examples.output: Guide to replacing hardcoded colors with semantic system colors (.systemBackground, .label, .secondaryLabel), creating asset catalog variants for custom colors, and testing the automatic Dark Mode adaptation.

best_practices: Use semantic system colors (.systemBackground, .label) instead of hardcoded RGB values to automatically support Dark Mode
Implement 44pt minimum touch targets and 8pt spacing grid for consistent, accessible layouts across all screen sizes
Respect user accessibility preferences by using @Environment values for Dynamic Type, Reduce Motion, and Bold Text
anti_patterns: Avoid hardcoding screen widths or assuming fixed device sizes - use safe areas and flexible layouts that adapt to all iPhone sizes
Do not use hamburger menus or override system back swipe gestures - follow platform conventions for navigation
Never request permissions at app launch without context - request permissions when user actually needs the feature

faq.question: What is the minimum touch target size for iOS?
faq.answer: 44x44 points for all interactive elements. This ensures buttons and controls are accessible to all users, including those with motor impairments.

faq.question: How do I support Dynamic Type in UIKit?
faq.answer: Use UIFont.preferredFont(forTextStyle:) with adjustsFontForContentSizeCategory = true, or UIFontMetrics for custom fonts to scale properly with system text size settings.

faq.question: Should I use UIKit or SwiftUI for new iOS projects?
faq.answer: SwiftUI is recommended for new projects when targeting iOS 14+. UIKit remains valuable for complex custom interfaces or when supporting older iOS versions.

faq.question: How do I handle Dark Mode in my app?
faq.answer: Use semantic system colors (.systemBackground, .label) which automatically adapt. For custom colors, create asset catalog variants for Any Appearance and Dark Appearance.

faq.question: What navigation pattern should I use?
faq.answer: Use TabView for 3-5 main sections, NavigationStack for drill-down hierarchies, and .sheet for focused tasks. Never use hamburger/drawer menus on iOS.

faq.question: How do I ensure my app works with VoiceOver?
faq.answer: Add accessibilityLabel to all interactive elements, use accessibilityHint for complex actions, set accessibilityTrait appropriately, and test with VoiceOver enabled regularly.
